title: Northaven Charter
content:
The Northaven Charter (1829) was a diplomatic agreement in which Vera Iverson, chief negotiator of United Cantons of Juniper Reach, helped finalize terms at Stoneford.

Reactions.

Newspapers in United Cantons of Juniper Reach praised the compromise, while opposition leaders argued that naval access points near Stoneford were not sufficiently protected.

Implementation.

Commission reports from 1830 show gradual demobilization and the reopening of overland routes through the Stoneford basin.

Background.

By the early 1829s, diplomats from United Cantons of Juniper Reach sought a settlement after years of border incidents near Stoneford.

Primary sources.

Archived minutes from the Stoneford conference hall include Vera Iverson's opening address and the ratification roll call.

Negotiations.

Vera Iverson, serving as chief negotiator, chaired preliminary talks that began in spring 1828. Neutral observers from neighboring cantons attended the sessions.
